---
title: "CYP1A2_genotyping"
format: html
---
# ============================================================
# CYP1A2 Genotype Distribution Visualisation
# Kuwaiti cohort (n=20) - Aldy v4.8.3 genotyping results
# ============================================================

```{r}
library(ggplot2)
library(dplyr)
library(tidyr)
library(scales)
library(kableExtra)
library(gt)

# ------------------------------------------------------------
# Colour-blind friendly palette (Okabe-Ito)
# Safe for deuteranopia, protanopia, and tritanopia
# ------------------------------------------------------------
okabe_ito <- c("#E69F00","#0072B2","#CC79A7", "#999999")
 
# ------------------------------------------------------------
# 1. Load data
# ------------------------------------------------------------
# Update the path below to point to your downloaded TSV file
cyp1a2 <- read.delim("all_samples_cyp1a2.tsv", sep = "\t", header = TRUE,
                      stringsAsFactors = FALSE)
 
# Quick check
str(cyp1a2)
table(cyp1a2$Genotype)
```

You can add options to executable code like this 

```{r}
# ------------------------------------------------------------
# 2. Genotype distribution (bar chart of counts)
# ------------------------------------------------------------
genotype_counts <- cyp1a2 %>%
  count(Genotype) %>%
  mutate(Percentage = n / sum(n) * 100)

n_genotypes <- nrow(genotype_counts)

genotype_plot <- ggplot(genotype_counts, 
                         aes(x = reorder(Genotype, -n), y = n, fill = Genotype)) +
  geom_col(width = 0.6, colour = "black", linewidth = 0.4) +
  geom_text(aes(label = paste0(n, " (", round(Percentage, 1), "%)")),
            vjust = -0.6, size = 4, family = "serif") +
  scale_fill_manual(values = okabe_ito[1:n_genotypes]) +
  labs(
    title = "CYP1A2 Genotype Distribution in Kuwaiti Cohort",
    subtitle = "n = 20 ALS cases — Aldy v4.8.3 genotyping",
    x = "Genotype",
    y = "Number of Samples"
  ) +
  scale_y_continuous(limits = c(0, max(genotype_counts$n) + 3),
                     breaks = scales::pretty_breaks()) +
  theme_classic(base_size = 14, base_family = "serif") +
  theme(
    legend.position = "none",
    plot.title      = element_text(face = "bold", hjust = 0.5, size = 14),
    plot.subtitle   = element_text(hjust = 0.5, size = 11, colour = "grey40"),
    axis.text.x     = element_text(face = "bold", size = 11),
    axis.text.y     = element_text(size = 10),
    axis.title      = element_text(size = 12),
    panel.grid.major.y = element_line(colour = "grey90", linewidth = 0.3)
  )

print(genotype_plot)
ggsave("cyp1a2_genotype_distribution.png", genotype_plot, 
       width = 7, height = 5, dpi = 300)
```


```{r}
# ------------------------------------------------------------
# 3. Allele frequency (split genotypes into individual alleles)
# ------------------------------------------------------------
alleles <- cyp1a2 %>%
  separate(Genotype, into = c("Allele1", "Allele2"), sep = "/") %>%
  pivot_longer(cols = c(Allele1, Allele2), values_to = "Allele") %>%
  count(Allele) %>%
  mutate(Frequency = n / sum(n) * 100)

n_alleles <- nrow(alleles)

allele_plot <- ggplot(alleles, 
                       aes(x = reorder(Allele, -Frequency), y = Frequency, fill = Allele)) +
  geom_col(width = 0.5, colour = "black", linewidth = 0.4) +
  geom_text(aes(label = paste0(round(Frequency, 1), "%")), 
            vjust = -0.6, size = 4, family = "serif") +
  scale_fill_manual(values = okabe_ito[1:n_alleles]) +
  labs(
    title = "CYP1A2 Allele Frequency in Kuwaiti Cohort",
    subtitle = paste0("n = ", sum(alleles$n), " alleles (20 individuals, 2 alleles each)"),
    x = "Allele",
    y = "Allele Frequency (%)"
  ) +
  scale_y_continuous(limits = c(0, max(alleles$Frequency) + 10),
                     breaks = scales::pretty_breaks()) +
  theme_classic(base_size = 14, base_family = "serif") +
  theme(
    legend.position = "none",
    plot.title      = element_text(face = "bold", hjust = 0.5, size = 14),
    plot.subtitle   = element_text(hjust = 0.5, size = 11, colour = "grey40"),
    axis.text.x     = element_text(face = "bold", size = 11),
    axis.text.y     = element_text(size = 10),
    axis.title      = element_text(size = 12),
    panel.grid.major.y = element_line(colour = "grey90", linewidth = 0.3)
  )

print(allele_plot)
ggsave("cyp1a2_allele_frequency.png", allele_plot, 
       width = 7, height = 5, dpi = 300)
```

```{r}
# ------------------------------------------------------------
# 4. Publication-style summary table
# ------------------------------------------------------------

summary_table <- data.frame(
  Genotype          = c("*1/*1", "*1/*30", "*30/*30"),
  `Sample Count`    = c(2, 7, 11),
  `Frequency (%)`   = c(10, 35, 55),
  check.names = FALSE
)

genotype_html <- summary_table %>%
  kbl(
    col.names = c("Genotype", "Sample Count", "Frequency (%)"),
    align     = c("l", "c", "c"),
    caption   = "Table. CYP1A2 genotype distribution in Kuwaiti ALS cohort"
  ) %>%
  kable_classic(full_width = FALSE, html_font = "Cambria") %>%
  column_spec(1, width = "6cm") %>%
  column_spec(2, width = "4cm") %>%
  column_spec(3, width = "4cm") %>%
  row_spec(0, bold = TRUE) %>%
  kableExtra::footnote(
    general = c(
      "Genotyping performed using Aldy v4.8.3.",
      "CYP1A2 is the primary enzyme responsible for riluzole metabolism."
    ),
    general_title     = "Note: ",
    footnote_as_chunk = TRUE,
    escape            = FALSE
  )

save_kable(genotype_html, "cyp1a2_genotype_summary_table.html")
cat("Saved: cyp1a2_genotype_summary_table.html\n")


```

```{r}



```
